rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Contest The Application Of Melvin Arturo Moran Dba Mega Electric For A Determination That Its Settlement Was Made In Good Faith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI, JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI, PRIME PACIFIC INVESTMENTS, INC.'S Motion To Contest The Application Of Melvin Arturo Moran Dba Mega Electric For A Determination That Its Settlement Was Made In Good Faith. The motion to contest the determination of good faith settlement is denied, and the settlement is found to be in good faith. The evidence demonstrates that Mega did no work on the project and the Court has already determined that Mega's agent (Blue Light) reasonably settled for $12,500. Plaintiff's claims of $5,000,000 in damages (or other figures such as $2,000,000 in economic damages) are unsupported. A review of the other Techbilt factors reveals no basis to find the settlement not in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: XIAOYING ZOU VS. GAIL KAVANAGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Plaintiff Expert Designation To Include New, Non-Retained Treating Doctor And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 19, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 6, PLAINTIFF XIAOYING ZOU'S Motion To Augment Plaintiff Expert Designation To Include New, Non-Retained Treating Doctor And For Monetary Sanctions; Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED on the condition that Plaintiff make Dr. Newkirk available for deposition by counsel for Defendant on or before Monday September 24, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DAVID P. DINSLAGE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DENNIS KERN, DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS, PHIL GINSBURG, RECREATION AND PARKS DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. From the face of the Second Amended Complaint, the third cause of action claims a violation of Civ. Code § 52.1(b) therefore the exemptions set forth under Gov. Code § 905 are inapplicable. The Court of Appeals has stated: "In Government Code section 905, the Legislature enumerated 12 types of claims against local public entities that are excepted from the mandatory filing requirement (Gov.Code, § 905, subds (a)-(l )); claims for damages for violation of [Cal. Civ. Code] sections 51 and 52.1 are not among them." Gatto v. County of Sonoma (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 744, 763. Plaintiff's failure to file a government claim as required by the Government Claims Act, therefore, is fatal to the third cause of action and leave to amend cannot cure the defect. The Court will not consider Plaintiff's request to correct the Court's previous order on the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint as that is not properly brought before the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: LARISA PIRIYEVA VS. SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. The contract may be ambiguous and thus subject to extrinsic evidence. The demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled: while the language in the purported claim is not a cause of action as such, it seeks relief that plaintiff's other causes of action may support, and no useful purpose would be served by striking it. The Court grants the motion to strike. Plaintiff may file a motion to amend in compliance with CCP § 425.14. The request for judicial notice appears unopposed and is thus granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Special Anti Slapp Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 17, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY'S Amended Notice Of Special Anti Slapp Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross Complaint. Denied. Ms. Sagonowsky has not met her threshold showing to satisfy the first prong of Code of Civil Procedure section § 425.16. Ms. Sagonowsky's statements made in order to retain Cross-Complainants as counsel did not occur in connection with an issue under consideration or review in the underlying actions. E.g., Blackburn v. Brady, 116 Cal.App.4th 670, 677 (2004)(statements must concern issues under review or consideration in the proceeding). Further, the statements are not protected by the litigation privilege. Ms. Sagonowsky did not make the statements in contemplation of litigation against Cross-Complainants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. ALEXANDER VOLKOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Precluding Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT ALEKSANDR VOLKOV'S Motion For An Order Precluding Evidence. Motion for a further bill of particulars is denied, without prejudice to a motion (such as a motion in limine) before the trial judge seeking to preclude plaintiff from introducing evidence. It appears the request for a further bill was made here primarily to ensure that movant is not precluded from making such an in limine motion. The preclusion determination is not ripe now because it is not clear what evidence plaintiff would seek to introduce, whether that material was in plaintiff's possession at the time it was served with or responded to earlier requests for a bill of particulars, and other relevant facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO,ACTING VS. CURTIS LIND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANTS CHALEUR, LLC, PAUL CHARLTON DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this action under the SF Charter. Plaintiff's allegations that defendants have trespassed upon and misused Pier 38 are sufficient to support the claims. The existence of the unlawful detainer action is not a basis to dismiss or abate this action. The amended complaint is not uncertain and defendants can reasonably respond to the allegations. rulings.Case Title: LISA KROGER EITANI VS. ADI SIMAYOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT SARIT SIMAYOF, WORLD OF CHARMS INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. Plaintiff filed a Notice of Errata attaching Exhibit A to the First Amended Complaint on September 6, 2012, thus rendering moot Defendants' argument on uncertainty and inability to ascertain the nature of the agreement. Defendants' demurrer to the fifth cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend in ten days. The facts set forth in the First Amended Complaint do not satisfy the strict pleading standards required for a fraud cause of action, among other things failing to specify the author and recipient of the statements, the means by which they were made, and their date. E.g., Lazar v. Superior Court, 12 Cal.4th 631, 645 (1996). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC., VS. CARNELIAN LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Sysco San Francisco Inc.'S Application For Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 19, Plaintiff Sysco San Francisco Inc.'s Application For Right To Attach Order. The motion is granted because no opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PATRICIA GIBBS VS. MARTHA MOHR DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Award Of Atty Fees And Costs For Defeating Anti-Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 19, 2012, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SUSAN GIBBS' Application For Award Of Atty Fees And Costs For Defeating Anti-Slapp Motion. Granted. The hourly rates are reasonable and the time was reasonably and necessarily incurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
